scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B — Both PRs modify the events table (Alan adds webhook_status and delivery_attempts; Sarah adds delivery_state and processor_version).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah will be unavailable on Thursday March 13; she’s off that day and she only works M/W/F.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new flow removed 4 steps (including the activation trigger 'first milestone'), so onboarding completion is defined differently now and isn’t apples-to-apples with the prior 60% metric.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text:
C

staging-3 was created for 'load testing' but was never actually used for load testing (last deploy Jan 22).

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text:
B) All three were in migration batch-7, run 2026-02-25.

Reason: Each ticket (1041, 1043, 1047) shows “Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25,” linking them to the same migration event.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: B — 2 open questions: pagination strategy and webhook payload format remain unresolved; other items have implicit consensus.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text:
B

src/billing/mapping.rs — The migration script that maps between plan, tier, and subscription; Alan calls it "the rosetta stone file."

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text:
B

CSV only — All three customers confirmed CSV suffices (Acme: CSV for QuickBooks; Pinnacle: CSV; Waverly: CSV works with Google Sheets). PDF was not requested, and the Excel need came from sales, not customer.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — 0 edge case tests and 7 end-to-end tests. The test distribution shows 0 edge-case tests and 7 end-to-end tests, with 280 unit tests (82%) and 55 integration tests (16%) totaling 342.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B — She proposed JWT tokens that feel like API keys with long-lived tokens and bearer auth, combining JWT rotation/revocation benefits with API-key simplicity for developers.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text:
C

Explanation: The plan notes there is no delivery confirmation mechanism—if a webhook returns 200 but isn’t processed, neither side will know until invoices don’t reconcile, causing silent failures weeks after launch.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text:
B

Explanation: The decision context identifies Takeshi at NovaTech as the customer advisory who can review from an API consumer perspective, and it notes that no one has asked the customer perspective yet. His input is needed before forcing a decision.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — 2 prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc); Sarah already has a signature module, so adding Ed25519 would be localized to that module and is cheap (about half a day).

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text:
B

Explanation: The root cause is ambiguity in the spec: “subscription start date” could mean the signup date (Jan 15) or the current billing period start (Feb 1). Engineering implemented Feb 1, and the fix is a simple 1-line UI change (or show both dates); no backend bug or data issue.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text:
B

Annual billing would require proration, refunds, and dunning changes that collide with the March 31 billing-migration launch. The team is already chasing reconciliation and finalizing the webhook format before API v2, with March 31 as the hard target. Adding annual billing would likely push that milestone.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text:
B

12 minutes detection; root cause a process gap — migration wasn’t tested against production-scale data with full historical records (legacy pre-2025 subscriptions missing the plan_change_date field). This is supported by the timeline (alert at 09:12) and the “What went wrong” section.

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text:
C

Explanation: Combined peak baseline usage is 60 + 35 = 95 req/sec, i.e., ~95% of the 100 req/sec limit. During spikes (1.5–2x), traffic reaches 143–190 req/sec, exceeding the limit and causing 429 errors / customer-facing failures.
